danger_signals.visual_form: Ecto energy bar gradually depletes on HUD
danger_signals.meaning: Player ghost losing power; imminent banishment to Underworld if fully depleted

danger_signals.visual_form: Bats, bouncing skulls, disembodied hands, steam vents rendered as hostile entities
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental threats that reduce Polterguy's health portrait in dungeon recovery stages

danger_signals.visual_form: Portrait in lower left corner visibly shrinks when damaged; reduces to eyes only near death
danger_signals.meaning: Critical health status; further damage ends game

danger_signals.visual_form: Grotesque blob monster transformation of family chihuahua after each house cleared
danger_signals.meaning: Boss encounter; significant threat requiring confrontation

danger_signals.visual_form: Family dog barks; decreases ecto tank
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental opposition; Sardini family pet actively drains player resources

safety_signals.visual_form: Shiny colored glowing indicators on interactive household objects (blue, orange, green)
safety_signals.meaning: Safe interaction points; objects available to possess and weaponize for scares

safety_signals.visual_form: Family member flees room; ecto tank partially recovers
Glowing ecto drops appear; star-shaped variant fully restores health
safety_signals.meaning: Resource recovery points; survival mechanics in dungeon stages

safety_signals.visual_form: Arrow points to exit if off-screen in Underworld
safety_signals.meaning: Navigation aid; path to safety and return to house

safety_signals.visual_form: Special spell drops from Underworld roof (disappear quickly)

faction_indicators.color_identity: Blue, Orange, Green (Fright 'em colors player controls)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Rebellious punk teenager ghost; skateboard accident victim
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Spectral form; isometric 3/4 perspective avatar; portrait health indicator in lower left

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Yuppie Italian family; business mogul Vito Sardini as primary target
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple family members with independent fear levels; family chihuahua transforms into Ecto Beast
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Occupants of four progressively complex houses; dog present as active resource drain

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Bats, bouncing skulls, disembodied hands, steam vents as hostile entities
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Twelve dungeon paths; recovery stage accessed when ecto depleted; repeating hazard cycles

world_state_to_visual.world_state: House occupied by Sardini family (primary game state)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Family members visible in rooms; household objects interactive with Fright 'em indicators; isometric home layout with multiple rooms and exits
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Family members patrol rooms; respond to scares by increasing fear level; flee house when fear reaches 'Very Scared'; dog barks to drain player ecto

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ecto energy bar empties; visual shift to Underworld dungeon aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Polterguy transported to Underworld; must collect ecto drops to recover and return to house at exact departure point; hazards become active threats

world_state_to_visual.world_state: House cleared of all family members (completion state)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Empty house; family chihuahua transforms into Ecto Beast visual
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounter triggered; Ecto Beast must be confronted; upon defeat, progression to next house

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Portrait shrinks to eyes only; visual indicator of imminent death
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: One additional hit from Underworld hazards ends game; player must immediately find ecto recovery

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Turn-based house haunting; simultaneous Underworld stage competition
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players alternate scare attempts until ecto depletion; head-to-head ecto collection race; first to finish gains next turn priority

environmental_storytelling.example: Household items (toy airplanes, severed hands, ghostly heads) reveal Polterguy's destructive haunting capability; ~400 possessible objects across four houses show escalating chaos

environmental_storytelling.technique: Spatial progression through four house iterations
environmental_storytelling.example: Four different Sardini family homes represent family relocation attempts to escape haunting; each house presumably more elaborate, showing escalation of both threat and player capability
Dungeon stages serve as resource recovery but also punishment; ecto depletion sends player to hostile supernatural realm, reinforcing ghost-human power economy

environmental_storytelling.technique: Fear meter as quantified emotional impact
environmental_storytelling.example: Family members' fear levels (rising to 'Very Scared') visualize Polterguy's psychological warfare effectiveness; meter communicates objective progress without dialogue

environmental_storytelling.technique: Ecto Beast transformation as story beat
environmental_storytelling.example: Family chihuahua grotesque mutation signals escalation; each house completion triggers new boss, suggesting supernatural corruption spreads

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves through rooms independently; maintains baseline presence
Reacts to scares; fear meter increases but doesn't flee
Flees house if in room with exit door; restores Polterguy ecto upon departure
Barks; actively drains Polterguy ecto as ambient threat
Transforms into Ecto Beast; becomes confrontable boss entity
Waits inside object; auto-activates scare when family member approaches
Remains in object until player button press; triggers on demand when family member near
Pilots possessed objects (toy airplanes, floating heads, severed hands) via D-pad; can trigger added effects (projectiles, movement attacks)
Autonomous movement patterns; collision damages Polterguy health; forces evasion gameplay

notes: Game communicates world-state primarily through visual depletion mechanics (ecto bar, health portrait) and spatial transitions (house-to-Underworld loop). Faction conflict is binary: punk ghost (player) vs. yuppie family (AI). Danger signals rely on environmental hazards and resource scarcity rather than explicit threat indicators. The Fright 'em color-coding system is core to visual communication of interaction types. Comedy-horror tone means danger signals are playful (possessed objects, family fleeing) rather than visceral. Two-player mode adds competitive resource-denial mechanic visualized through turn order. Narrative of revenge against business mogul expressed through environmental destruction rather than cutscenes.
